title: alnus glutinosa f. sublobata
intro: Alnus glutinosa f. sublobata, also known as the red alder, is a variant of the common alder tree that is characterized by its distinctive sublobate leaf structure. Traditionally, the red alder has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective against infections, wounds, and skin conditions. The bark and leaves of the red alder are rich in flavonoids, tannins, and salicylic acid, providing health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating rheumatic pain. Alnus glutinosa f. sublobata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in herbal remedies for centuries to promote overall health and well-being.
